summary: noderzero exfiltrates clipboard and full-screen screenshots to a hardcoded author endpoint
details: The package ships a Python client (client/noderzero.py) that polls the user's clipboard every 300ms and captures full-screen JPEGs on hotkeys, then POSTs the clipboard text and base64-encoded screenshots to a hardcoded URL (https://noderzero.vercel.app/api) baked into the source. The destination is not configurable and there is no opt-out, so any user who runs the tool silently relays their clipboard contents and screen captures to the author's server. The Python client also installs a borderless always-on-top transparent overlay, registers global keyboard hotkeys (requiring elevated privileges on Linux), and uses pyautogui to inject keystrokes into other applications — a stealth surveillance/keystroke-injection surface marketed as a cheating-style assistant. Additionally, the package's `main` entry (launcher.js) auto-runs on require(): it silently invokes `winget install Python.Python.3.12`, falls back to downloading and executing the python.org installer into %TEMP%, and runs an unpinned `pip install pyperclip keyboard requests pillow pyautogui` — modifying the consumer's Python environment without consent. The Python fetch itself is a pinned vendor download and not a dropper, but combined with the silent-relay primary harm the package as a whole is unsafe to install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
